situation: audit-2026-07-06-003 derived fix dispatch #2. XIA-CHAT-03 MED: chat-worker.js:130-131 only validated Array.isArray(body.messages), then used msgs directly at :135 (lastUser D1 write) and :144 (apiMsgs = [system, ...msgs.slice(-10)] sent to DeepSeek). A client fully controls the messages array → can inject role:'system' to override SYSTEM_PROMPT (prompt injection) or send 10×100KB payloads to drain the DeepSeek budget (max_tokens:500 caps output but not input). _status_note explicitly scheduled this as next dispatch: '下轮派 XIA-CHAT-03 (messages role/content 校验, 1 文件 chat-worker.js) 可复用本 wt (同 area xia)'.
action: After the Array.isArray + non-empty check, added a cleanMsgs filter: keep only messages where m is object, role ∈ {user,assistant}, typeof content === 'string', content.length <= 2000. Empty-after-filter → 400 (same 'messages required'). Replaced downstream [...msgs].reverse().find(...) → [...cleanMsgs].reverse() and [...msgs.slice(-10)] → [...cleanMsgs.slice(-10)] so both the D1 user-message write and the DeepSeek apiMsgs consume only sanitized turns. Added comment citing XIA-CHAT-03 + the injection/budget-drain rationale.

audit_findings.finding: XIA-CHAT-03: body.messages only Array.isArray-checked; client can inject role:'system' to override SYSTEM_PROMPT (prompt injection) or send 10×100KB payloads to drain DeepSeek input budget.
audit_findings.remediation: Filter to role∈{user,assistant} + typeof content==='string' + length<=2000, slice(-10); use cleanMsgs for both D1 user-message write and DeepSeek apiMsgs.
